question: Named storm before start of Atlantic hurricane season
textDescription:
Resolution criteria

The 2026 Atlantic hurricane season officially begins on June 1, 2026. This market resolves YES if a named storm forms in the Atlantic Basin before June 1, 2026 (UTC). Resolution will be determined by advisories  from the National Hurricane Center (https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/).
newsShortReason: No named storms have formed in the Atlantic Basin before June 1, 2026.
newsKeyEvidence: NOAA forecasts a below-average Atlantic hurricane season with 8 to 14 named storms, 3 to 6 hurricanes, and 1 to 3 major hurricanes, attributing this to expected El Niño conditions. | The National Hurricane Center has not issued any advisories for named storms in the Atlantic Basin as of May 27, 2026. | Historically, the first named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season typically forms in June, with the first storm usually arriving by June 20. | In recent years, the first named storms have formed in June, such as Tropical Storm Alberto on June 17, 2024, and Tropical Storm Andrea on June 23, 2025. | Sea surface temperatures in the Gulf of Mexico are currently the third warmest on record for May, which could potentially lead to early-season storm development. | However, the development of a strong El Niño is expected to suppress hurricane activity, making early-season storms less likely.
